text.content: I'm a non-technical managing partner. Last week I ran 2 AI agents 24/7 for my actual job. Here's what I learned about cost, reliability, and sanity. 🧵
The setup: Molty 🦎 (coordinator agent) handles my inbox, calendar, Discord. Raphael 🔴 (corporate agent) drafts proposals, monitors deal flow. Both run on Railway.

Cost breakdown after 7 days:
• Railway hosting: $12
• Claude API (Sonnet): $47
• Total: ~$59/week ($8.40/day)

That's cheaper than one hour of a junior employee.
What surprised me: the agents work best when they DON'T try to be smart. Simple rules, clear handoffs, explicit memory files. No magic.
Biggest fail: I gave Molty a vague instruction ("help me with emails"). It hallucinated context and sent a weird reply. Lesson: specificity > speed.
What works: daily standups. Every day at 5PM, Molty summarizes what happened, what's pending, asks for decisions. Forces me to stay in the loop.
The real unlock isn't automation—it's leverage. I'm doing the same work but with 2 extra brains that don't forget, don't get tired, and cost $8/day.

Will I keep going? Yes. The ROI is obvious. Next: adding Leonardo (research agent) and Donatello (dev agent).

If you're building something similar, DM me. Happy to share the stack. #AIAgents #BuildInPublic
